content: Just as language learning reflection (the common educational definition of reflexion) requires learners to review past mistakes, adjust expression logic and consolidate correct language habits from multiple attempts, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can borrow the structured scaffolding of high school language writing reflection workshops to build more systematic agentic self-reflection cycles for autonomous Reflexion agents. This structured, scaffolded reflection framework turns vague LLM self-critique into step-by-step error diagnosis similar to how students revise essays, which can improve the consistency of self-reflection outputs.
